formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Unfortunately, the chapter 'Doping — Drugs in Sports' in CBSE Class 12 Physical Education is more focused on the concept of doping, its types, effects, detection methods, and related laws rather than mathematical formulas.

However, I'll create a relevant JSON array with key statements/laws and some relevant information for your reference:

[
  {
    "name": "The World Anti-Doping Code",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The World Anti-Doping Code is a set of rules and guidelines that govern the use of performance-enhancing substances in sports."
  },
  {
    "name": "The WADA Prohibited List",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The WADA Prohibited List is an annual publication that outlines the substances and methods prohibited in sports."
  },
  {
    "name": "The IOC Anti-Doping Rules",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The IOC Anti-Doping Rules are the anti-doping regulations that govern the Olympic Games."
  },
  {
    "name": "The Anabolic-Androgenic Steroid (AAS) Effect",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "AAS can increase muscle mass, strength, and aggression, but can also have negative effects on the body, such as liver damage and cardiovascular disease."
  },
  {
    "name": "The Doping Control Process",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The doping control process involves urine or blood testing, sample collection, and analysis to detect the presence of prohibited substances."
  },
  {
    "name": "The Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) System",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The TUE system allows athletes to use prohibited substances for medical purposes, subject to approval from the relevant authorities."
  }
]

Please note that this chapter does not have any mathematical formulas. If you need more information or have any other questions, feel free to ask!
